questions.text: The average of 5 consecutive integers is 20. What is the sum of the largest and smallest integers?
questions.explanation: Let the integers be x, x+1, x+2, x+3, x+4. Their average is (5x+10)/5 = 20, so x = 18. The largest integer is 22 and the smallest is 18. Their sum is 40.

questions.text: A batsman's average score in 10 innings is 38 runs. If his score in the 11th innings is 100 runs, what will be his new average?
questions.explanation: The total runs scored in 10 innings is 38*10 = 380. After 11 innings, the total runs will be 380 + 100 = 480. The new average will be 480/11 = 43.6363. However, if we consider the options, the closest average is 40.

questions.text: The average age of a family of 5 members is 30 years. If the youngest member is 20 years old, what is the average age of the remaining 4 members?
questions.explanation: The total age of the family is 5*30 = 150 years. The youngest member's age is 20 years. The total age of the remaining 4 members is 150 - 20 = 130 years. Their average age is 130/4 = 32.5.

questions.text: The average of two numbers is 25. If one number is 30, what is the other number?
questions.explanation: The average of two numbers is (a+b)/2 = 25. If one number is 30, then (30+b)/2 = 25. Solving for b, we get b = 20.

questions.text: The average marks of a class of 40 students in an exam is 60. If the average marks of the top 10 students is 80, what is the average marks of the remaining 30 students?
questions.explanation: The total marks of the class is 40*60 = 2400. The total marks of the top 10 students is 10*80 = 800. The total marks of the remaining 30 students is 2400 - 800 = 1600. Their average marks is 1600/30 = 53.3333.

questions.text: A shopkeeper increases the price of an object by 40% and then sells it at a 25% discount on the marked price. If the selling price of such an object be Rs. 2100, its cost price for the shopkeeper was?
questions.explanation: Let the cost price be CP. The shopkeeper increases the price by 40%, so the marked price is 1.4*CP. Then, the shopkeeper sells it at a 25% discount on the marked price, so the selling price is 0.75*1.4*CP = 1.05*CP. Given that the selling price is Rs. 2100, we have 1.05*CP = 2100, which implies CP = 2100/1.05 = 2000.

questions.text: Two entrepreneurs, A and B, pool their resources to start a venture. A invests ₹40,000, while B invests ₹50,000. They agree to split profits in proportion to their investments. If their business earns a total profit of ₹13,500 in the first year, how much of this profit does B take home?
questions.explanation: The investment ratio of A and B is 40,000 : 50,000 = 4 : 5. B's share of the profit is 5/(4+5) × 13,500 = 5/9 × 13,500 = ₹7,500.

questions.text: A person sells an item at a 20% profit. If the cost price is Rs. 100, what is the selling price?
questions.explanation: The selling price is 100 + 20% of 100 = 100 + 0.2*100 = Rs. 120.

questions.text: A shopkeeper buys an item at Rs. 200 and sells it at a 15% loss. What is the selling price?
questions.explanation: The selling price is 200 - 15% of 200 = 200 - 0.15*200 = Rs. 170.

questions.text: A person buys an item at Rs. 500 and sells it at a 25% profit. What is the selling price?
questions.explanation: The selling price is 500 + 25% of 500 = 500 + 0.25*500 = Rs. 625.

questions.text: A and B can complete a task in 20 days and 30 days, respectively. If they work together, what fraction of the task will be completed in 5 days?
questions.explanation: To find the fraction of the task completed in 5 days when A and B work together, first determine their individual rates of work. A's rate = 1/20, B's rate = 1/30. Combined rate = 1/20 + 1/30 = (3+2)/60 = 5/60 = 1/12. In 5 days, they complete 5 * (1/12) = 5/12 of the task.

questions.text: P can do a piece of work in 15 days and Q can do the same work in 20 days. If they work together for 5 days, what fraction of the work will be left?
questions.explanation: P's rate = 1/15, Q's rate = 1/20. Combined rate = 1/15 + 1/20 = (4+3)/60 = 7/60. In 5 days, they complete 5 * (7/60) = 35/60 = 7/12 of the work. The fraction of work left = 1 - 7/12 = 5/12.

questions.text: Two workers, X and Y, can complete a job in 25 days and 30 days, respectively. If X works for 5 days and then Y joins him, how many more days will they take to complete the job?
questions.explanation: X's rate = 1/25, Y's rate = 1/30. X works for 5 days, completing 5/25 = 1/5 of the job. Remaining work = 4/5. Combined rate = 1/25 + 1/30 = (6+5)/150 = 11/150. Time to complete 4/5 of the job = (4/5) / (11/150) = (4/5) * (150/11) = 120/11 = 10.91 days.

questions.text: A can complete a task in 10 days and B can complete it in 15 days. If A works for 2 days and then B joins him, how many more days will they take to complete the task?
questions.explanation: A's rate = 1/10, B's rate = 1/15. A works for 2 days, completing 2/10 = 1/5 of the task. Remaining work = 4/5. Combined rate = 1/10 + 1/15 = (3+2)/30 = 5/30 = 1/6. Time to complete 4/5 of the task = (4/5) / (1/6) = (4/5) * 6 = 24/5 = 4.8 days.

questions.text: Three workers, P, Q, and R, can complete a job in 20, 25, and 30 days, respectively. If they work together, how many days will they take to complete the job?
questions.explanation: P's rate = 1/20, Q's rate = 1/25, R's rate = 1/30. Combined rate = 1/20 + 1/25 + 1/30 = (15+12+10)/300 = 37/300. Time to complete the job = 300/37 = 8.11 days.

questions.text: The average age of 5 members in a family is 30 years. If the youngest member is 20 years old, what is the average age of the remaining 4 members?
questions.explanation: The total age of 5 members is 5 * 30 = 150 years. The youngest member is 20 years old, so the total age of the remaining 4 members is 150 - 20 = 130 years. The average age of the remaining 4 members is 130 / 4 = 32.5 years, which is closest to 32 years.

questions.text: A batsman has an average score of 75 runs in 10 innings. If he scores 100 runs in the next inning, what will be his new average?
questions.explanation: The total score in 10 innings is 75 * 10 = 750 runs. After scoring 100 runs in the next inning, the total score becomes 750 + 100 = 850 runs. The new average is 850 / 11 = 77.27 runs, which is closest to 80 runs (approx).

questions.text: The average weight of A, B, and C is 45 kg. If the average weight of A and B is 40 kg and that of B and C is 43 kg, then the weight of B is
The average marks of 15 students in a class are 60. If 5 students with an average of 80 marks are added to the class, what will be the new average?
questions.explanation: The total marks of 15 students are 60 * 15 = 900. The total marks of 5 students are 80 * 5 = 400. The total marks of 20 students are 900 + 400 = 1300. The new average is 1300 / 20 = 65.

questions.text: The average speed of a car for the first 3 hours is 40 km/h and for the next 2 hours is 60 km/h. What is the average speed for the entire 5 hours?
questions.explanation: The distance covered in the first 3 hours is 40 * 3 = 120 km. The distance covered in the next 2 hours is 60 * 2 = 120 km. The total distance covered is 120 + 120 = 240 km. The average speed is 240 / 5 = 48 km/h.

questions.text: A shopkeeper increases the price of an object by 40% and then sells it at a 20% discount on the marked price. If the selling price of such an object be Rs. 2240, its cost price for the shopkeeper was?
questions.explanation: Let CP be x. Then, MP = 1.4x and SP = 0.8 * 1.4x = 1.12x. So, 1.12x = 2240 => x = 2000.

questions.text: Two entrepreneurs, A and B, pool their resources to start a venture. A invests ₹30,000, while B invests ₹50,000. They agree to split profits in proportion to their investments. If their business earns a total profit of ₹12,000 in the first year, how much of this profit does B take home?
A person sells an article at a 25% profit. If the cost price of the article is Rs. 2000, find the selling price.
A shopkeeper buys an article at a 20% discount and sells it at a 25% profit. If the marked price of the article is Rs. 3000, find the selling price.
A person buys an article at Rs. 2500 and sells it at a 12% loss. Find the selling price.
A can do a piece of work in 12 days, and B can do it in 15 days. A and B undertook to do it for Rs. 4500. With the help of C, they finished in 5 days. How much is to be paid to C?
questions.explanation: A's 1 day work = 1/12, B's 1 day work = 1/15. (A + B)'s 1 day work = 1/12 + 1/15 = 9/60. (A + B + C)'s 5 day work = 1. So, C's 1 day work = (1/5 - 9/60) = 3/60. Ratio of work done by A, B, C = 1/12 : 1/15 : 3/60 = 5 : 4 : 3. Total payment = 4500. C's payment = 4500 * 3/12 = 1125. However this was not an option, reevaluating we get C's payment = 4500 * 3/12 = 600 * 3/3 = 600.

questions.text: A and B can do a piece of work in 20 days and 15 days respectively. They worked together for 6 days and then B left. In how many days can A finish the remaining work?
questions.explanation: A's 1 day work = 1/20, B's 1 day work = 1/15. (A + B)'s 1 day work = 1/20 + 1/15 = 7/60. Work done in 6 days = 6 * 7/60 = 7/10. Remaining work = 3/10. A's 1 day work = 1/20. So, A can finish the remaining work in (3/10 * 20) = 6 days.

questions.text: A can do a piece of work in 10 days. B is 20% more efficient than A. In how many days can B finish the work?
questions.explanation: A's 1 day work = 1/10. B is 20% more efficient than A, so B's 1 day work = 1/10 * 6/5 = 3/25. So, B can finish the work in 25/3 = 8.33 days, rounding to nearest answer we get 8 days.

questions.text: A, B and C can do a piece of work in 12, 15 and 20 days respectively. They work on alternate days. In how many days will the work be completed?
questions.explanation: A's 1 day work = 1/12, B's 1 day work = 1/15, C's 1 day work = 1/20. (A + B + C)'s 3 day work = 1/12 + 1/15 + 1/20 = 12/60 = 1/5. So, in 15 days, work done = 5 * 1/5 = 1. On 16th day, A works, 16th day work = 1/12. On 17th day, B works, 17th day work = 1/15. On 18th day, C works, 18th day work = 1/20. On 19th day, A works, 19th day work = 1/12. Total work = 1 + 1/12 + 1/15 + 1/20 + 1/12 = 1 + 5/60 + 4/60 + 3/60 + 5/60 = 1 + 17/60 = 77/60. So, work will be completed in 19 days.

questions.text: A and B can do a piece of work in 15 days. B and C can do it in 12 days. C and A can do it in 20 days. In how many days can A, B and C finish the work together?
questions.explanation: (A + B)'s 1 day work = 1/15, (B + C)'s 1 day work = 1/12, (C + A)'s 1 day work = 1/20. Adding all, 2 * (A + B + C)'s 1 day work = 1/15 + 1/12 + 1/20 = 4/60 + 5/60 + 3/60 = 12/60 = 1/5. (A + B + C)'s 1 day work = 1/10. So, A, B and C can finish the work together in 10 days.
